package router

import (
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
	"net/http"
)

// Positive scenario for route_registration_contains_business_logic
// Anti-pattern: Route handlers contain business logic (DB queries, calculations) instead of delegating to services.
func SetupRouter(db *gorm.DB) *gin.Engine {
	r := gin.Default()

	// Anti-pattern: business logic (DB query) directly in route handler
	r.GET("/users", func(c *gin.Context) {
		var users []struct {
			ID    uint   `json:"id"`
			Name  string `json:"name"`
			Email string `json:"email"`
		}
		db.Where("status = ?", "active").Order("created_at DESC").Limit(100).Find(&users)
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"users": users})
	})

	// Anti-pattern: business logic (DB insert + validation) directly in route handler
	r.POST("/users", func(c *gin.Context) {
		var body struct {
			Name  string `json:"name" binding:"required"`
			Email string `json:"email" binding:"required"`
		}
		if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&body); err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
			return
		}

		var count int64
		db.Model(&struct{}{}).Where("email = ?", body.Email).Count(&count)
		if count > 0 {
			c.JSON(http.StatusConflict, gin.H{"error": "email already exists"})
			return
		}

		c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, gin.H{"name": body.Name, "email": body.Email})
	})

	// Anti-pattern: business logic (DB update + calculation) directly in route handler
	r.PUT("/users/:id", func(c *gin.Context) {
		id := c.Param("id")
		var body struct {
			Name string `json:"name"`
		}
		if err := c.ShouldBindJSON(&body); err != nil {
			c.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, gin.H{"error": err.Error()})
			return
		}

		db.Where("id = ?", id).Updates(map[string]interface{}{"name": body.Name})
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"id": id, "name": body.Name, "updated": true})
	})

	return r
}
